question.text: As per the L.C. Gupta Committee, what is the maximum percentage of Trading Members that can serve on the governing council of a derivatives exchange?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The L.C. Gupta Committee recommended that not more than 40% of the governing council of a derivative exchange should consist of Trading Members. This was to ensure independent governance and prevent conflict of interest. **The Trap:** 50% might seem logical as 'half', but the committee deliberately set it lower at 40% to ensure non-trading/public interest representation exceeds trading member representation. **Cross-Exam Context:** This governance norm applies to the exchange-level structure. It complements other L.C. Gupta recommendations like minimum 50 members and SEBI-approved clearing corporation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Independent governance of exchanges was a key concern — the 40% cap ensures that regulatory decisions at the exchange level are not dominated by trading interests.
